name: Old Catalan

ancestors.notes: Descent from Late/Tarraconensis Vulgar Latin; Catalan is a transitional Gallo-Ibero-Romance variety.
Iberian (non-IE) substrate of the eastern Iberian coast, plus a Celtiberian layer inland.
Visigothic (Gothic) superstrate: onomastic and lexical Germanic layer shared with the rest of Hispania.
Andalusi Arabic adstrate: al- loanwords (alberginia, safareig, raval), lighter than in Castilian.
Occitan adstrate: close Gallo-Romance sister; strong medieval literary and lexical convergence (troubadour koine).

allophone_rules.notes: Word-final βü®-igβü© is the voiceless palato-alveolar affricate [t É] (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.1; IEC orthography ch. 2): vaig [Υàbat É], mig [Υàmit É], desig [d…ôΥàzit É], passeig [p…ôΥàs…¦t É]. The βü®iβü© is part of the digraph and is absorbed by the affricate, so the glide it would otherwise contribute after another vowel is deleted.

Word-final βü®-igβü© is the voiceless palato-alveolar affricate [t É] (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.1; IEC orthography ch. 2): vaig [Υàbat É], mig [Υàmit É], desig [d…ôΥàzit É], passeig [p…ôΥàs…¦t É]. The βü®iβü© is part of the digraph and is absorbed by the affricate, so the glide it would otherwise contribute after another vowel is deleted.

The CONSERVATIVE (Old-Catalan) outcome of a word-final βü®-nt/-lt/-mp/-ncβü© cluster: the stop SURVIVES, devoiced βÄî molt [Υàmolt], camp [Υàkamp], sang [Υàsa≈΄k], profund [p…ΨoΥàfunt] (Moll 2006; Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3, ¬ß10.4). VALENCIAN and BALEARIC still have it and therefore declare nothing; CENTRAL, NORTH-WESTERN and NORTHERN innovated and override this rule by id with the deletion (surface ""). The rule is stated here, rather than leaving the segment to the general final-devoicing rules below, because this is the exact segment the isogloss runs through: an inherited rule can be overridden by id but not switched off, so the innovation has to live in the rule the innovators replace, not in one they append.

The CONSERVATIVE (Old-Catalan) outcome of a word-final βü®-nt/-lt/-mp/-ncβü© cluster: the stop SURVIVES, devoiced βÄî molt [Υàmolt], camp [Υàkamp], sang [Υàsa≈΄k], profund [p…ΨoΥàfunt] (Moll 2006; Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3, ¬ß10.4). VALENCIAN and BALEARIC still have it and therefore declare nothing; CENTRAL, NORTH-WESTERN and NORTHERN innovated and override this rule by id with the deletion (surface ""). The rule is stated here, rather than leaving the segment to the general final-devoicing rules below, because this is the exact segment the isogloss runs through: an inherited rule can be overridden by id but not switched off, so the innovation has to live in the rule the innovators replace, not in one they append.

VALENCIAN and BALEARIC still have it and therefore declare nothing; CENTRAL, NORTH-WESTERN and NORTHERN innovated and override this rule by id with the deletion (surface ""). The rule is stated here, rather than leaving the segment to the general final-devoicing rules below, because this is the exact segment the isogloss runs through: an inherited rule can be overridden by id but not switched off, so the innovation has to live in the rule the innovators replace, not in one they append.

PRE-CONSONANTAL cluster simplification: a stop is deleted between a homorganic nasal or lateral and a following consonant βÄî here the plural βü®-sβü© (importants [impu…ΨΥàtans], tants [Υàtans], cruiximents [k…Ψu ÉiΥàmens]). Unlike the deletion of an ABSOLUTELY word-final stop, which is a Central/North-Western innovation, this one is pan-Catalan: the 4catac expert transcriptions have it in all four varieties, Valencian and Balearic included (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß10.4; Recasens 1993; Veny 1982 ch. 3). Its cross-word counterpart is the CA_CLUSTER_PRECONS_* sandhi rule.

PRE-CONSONANTAL cluster simplification: a stop is deleted between a homorganic nasal or lateral and a following consonant βÄî here the plural βü®-sβü© (importants [impu…ΨΥàtans], tants [Υàtans], cruiximents [k…Ψu ÉiΥàmens]). Unlike the deletion of an ABSOLUTELY word-final stop, which is a Central/North-Western innovation, this one is pan-Catalan: the 4catac expert transcriptions have it in all four varieties, Valencian and Balearic included (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß10.4; Recasens 1993; Veny 1982 ch. 3). Its cross-word counterpart is the CA_CLUSTER_PRECONS_* sandhi rule.

PRE-CONSONANTAL cluster simplification: a stop is deleted between a homorganic nasal or lateral and a following consonant βÄî here the plural βü®-sβü© (importants [impu…ΨΥàtans], tants [Υàtans], cruiximents [k…Ψu ÉiΥàmens]). Unlike the deletion of an ABSOLUTELY word-final stop, which is a Central/North-Western innovation, this one is pan-Catalan: the 4catac expert transcriptions have it in all four varieties, Valencian and Balearic included (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß10.4; Recasens 1993; Veny 1982 ch. 3). Its cross-word counterpart is the CA_CLUSTER_PRECONS_* sandhi rule.
Final-obstruent devoicing (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3; Recasens 1993): word-final /b/ βÜ£ [p].
Final-obstruent devoicing (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3): word-final /d/ βÜ£ [t] (fred [Υàf…Ψet]).
Final-obstruent devoicing (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3): word-final /…Γ/ βÜ£ [k].
Final-obstruent devoicing (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3): word-final /z/ βÜ£ [s].
Final-obstruent devoicing (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3): word-final / £/ βÜ£ [ É].
Final-obstruent devoicing (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3): word-final /v/ βÜ£ [f].
Final-obstruent devoicing (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3): the word-final affricate /d £/ βÜ£ [t É] (jutge is [Υàd £ud £…ô] but mig [Υàmit É]).
Final-obstruent devoicing (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3): word-final /dz/ βÜ£ [ts].
Nasal place assimilation (Recasens 1993; Wheeler 2005 ¬ß10.3): /n/ βÜ£ [m] before a labial consonant.
Nasal place assimilation (Recasens 1993; Wheeler 2005 ¬ß10.3): /n/ βÜ£ [≈΄] before a velar consonant.

Nasal place assimilation to a PALATO-ALVEOLAR (Recasens 1993; Wheeler 2005 ¬ß10.3): /n/ βÜ£ […≤] before / É  £ t É d £  é/ βÄî Ο†ngel [Υàa…≤ £…ôl], un jutge [u…≤ Υàd £ud £…ô], any llunyΟ† [Υàa…≤  éuΥà…≤a]. Sits with the labial and velar assimilation rules; the palato-alveolar context is the one Catalan adds to the familiar labial/velar pair.

Pre-lateral GEMINATION blocks spirantization: /b/ and /…Γ/ before a lateral geminate rather than lenite βÄî poble [Υàp…îbΥêl…ô], regla [Υàre…ΓΥêl…ô], not *[Υàp…îΈ≤l…ô] (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2, ¬ß10.2; Recasens 1993). The rule is an identity rewrite placed BEFORE the spirantization rules: first match wins, so the stop survives. The gemination itself (length) is not written, only the stop quality.

Pre-lateral GEMINATION blocks spirantization: /b/ and /…Γ/ before a lateral geminate rather than lenite βÄî poble [Υàp…îbΥêl…ô], regla [Υàre…ΓΥêl…ô], not *[Υàp…îΈ≤l…ô] (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2, ¬ß10.2; Recasens 1993). The rule is an identity rewrite placed BEFORE the spirantization rules: first match wins, so the stop survives. The gemination itself (length) is not written, only the stop quality.

Post-vocalic spirantization (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2; Recasens 1993): /b/ βÜ£ the approximant [Έ≤] after a vowel βÄî roba [Υàr…îΈ≤…ô], sobre [ΥàsoΈ≤…Ψ…ô]. Blocked word-finally, where the devoicing rules above fire first.
Post-vocalic spirantization (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2; Recasens 1993): /d/ βÜ£ [ΟΑ] after a vowel (cada [ΥàkaΟΑ…ô]).
Post-vocalic spirantization (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2; Recasens 1993): /…Γ/ βÜ£ […Θ] after a vowel (pagar [p…ôΥà…Θa]).
Post-vocalic spirantization of the βü®guβü© = […Γw] trie key (aigua [Υàaj…Θw…ô]); same rule as CA_SPIRANT_G.
Post-vocalic spirantization of the βü®guβü© = […Γu] trie key before a consonant (recorregut [r…ôkur…ôΥà…Θut]); same rule as CA_SPIRANT_G.

Post-continuant spirantization (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2): /b d …Γ/ lenite to [Έ≤ ΟΑ …Θ] after any continuant, not only after a vowel βÄî arbres [Υàa…ΨΈ≤…Ψ…ôs], serveix [s…ôrΥàΈ≤…¦ É]. Complements the post-vocalic rule; /d/ is excluded after a lateral, where it stays a stop.

Post-continuant spirantization (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2): /b d …Γ/ lenite to [Έ≤ ΟΑ …Θ] after any continuant, not only after a vowel βÄî arbres [Υàa…ΨΈ≤…Ψ…ôs], serveix [s…ôrΥàΈ≤…¦ É]. Complements the post-vocalic rule; /d/ is excluded after a lateral, where it stays a stop.

Post-continuant spirantization (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2): /b d …Γ/ lenite to [Έ≤ ΟΑ …Θ] after any continuant, not only after a vowel βÄî arbres [Υàa…ΨΈ≤…Ψ…ôs], serveix [s…ôrΥàΈ≤…¦ É]. Complements the post-vocalic rule; /d/ is excluded after a lateral, where it stays a stop.

Post-continuant spirantization (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2): /b d …Γ/ lenite to [Έ≤ ΟΑ …Θ] after any continuant, not only after a vowel βÄî arbres [Υàa…ΨΈ≤…Ψ…ôs], serveix [s…ôrΥàΈ≤…¦ É]. Complements the post-vocalic rule; /d/ is excluded after a lateral, where it stays a stop.
Post-continuant spirantization of /d/ (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2). Unlike /b/ and /…Γ/, /d/ does NOT lenite after a lateral (or a nasal): el dia keeps the stop.

βü®xβü© in the learned prefix ex- before a vowel is the voiced cluster […Γz] (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.1; IEC orthography ch. 2): examen […ô…ΓΥàzam…ôn], exercici […ô…Γz…ôrΥàsisi], exacte […ô…ΓΥàzakt…ô]. Elsewhere between vowels βü®xβü© stays [ks] (mΟ†xim [Υàmaksim]).

sandhi_rules.name: precons-cluster-t
sandhi_rules.notes: PRE-CONSONANTAL cluster simplification across a word boundary: the stop of a final βü®-nt/-lt/-mp/-ncβü© cluster is deleted before a following consonant in EVERY Catalan variety, including the ones that keep it before a vowel or a pause βÄî the 4catac expert transcriptions have Valencian and Balearic molt alta [Υàmolt Υàalta] (stop kept before a vowel) but el vint de juny [el Υàvin de βÄΠ] and tant que [Υàta≈΄ ke] (stop deleted before a consonant), while Central and North-Western have [Υàmol Υàalt…ô] in both (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß10.4; Recasens 1993). Declared here, in the common core, because it is pan-Catalan; it is inert in Central and North-Western, where the word-level CA_CLUSTER_*_FINAL rules have already removed the stop. Listed before the voicing rules so the stop is deleted rather than voiced.

sandhi_rules.name: final-ps-voicing
sandhi_rules.notes: External (cross-word) VOICING ASSIMILATION of a word-final obstruent: Catalan word-final obstruents are voiceless in isolation and before a voiceless segment, but voice before a following vowel or voiced consonant inside the phonological phrase (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3, ¬ß10.5; Recasens 1993): els amics […ôlz …ôΥàmiks], coses importants [Υàk…îz…ôz impurΥàtans], cops havΟ≠em [Υàk…îbz …ôΥàΈ≤i…ôm], vaig obrir [Υàbad £ uΥàΈ≤…Ψi]. Longer clusters are listed first so the whole cluster voices.

sandhi_rules.notes: External (cross-word) VOICING ASSIMILATION of a word-final obstruent: Catalan word-final obstruents are voiceless in isolation and before a voiceless segment, but voice before a following vowel or voiced consonant inside the phonological phrase (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.3, ¬ß10.5; Recasens 1993): els amics […ôlz …ôΥàmiks], coses importants [Υàk…îz…ôz impurΥàtans], cops havΟ≠em [Υàk…îbz …ôΥàΈ≤i…ôm], vaig obrir [Υàbad £ uΥàΈ≤…Ψi]. Longer clusters are listed first so the whole cluster voices. A single word-final STOP voices only before a voiced CONSONANT βÄî before a vowel it stays voiceless (poc a poc [Υàp…îk …ô Υàp…îk], soc un [Υàs…îk un]), unlike a sibilant, which voices before a vowel too.

sandhi_rules.name: external-spirantization-b
sandhi_rules.notes: PHRASE-LEVEL (cross-word) SPIRANTIZATION: Catalan lenition of /b d …Γ/ is not word-bounded βÄî inside a phonological phrase an initial /b d …Γ/ surfaces as the approximant [Έ≤ ΟΑ …Θ] whenever the preceding word ends in a continuant (a vowel, glide, liquid or fricative), and stays a stop after a pause, a nasal or another stop (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2, ¬ß10.5; Recasens 1993): la seva germana [l…ô ΥàseΈ≤…ô  £…ôrΥàman…ô], de decidir [ΟΑ…ô ΟΑ…ôsiΥàΟΑi], es va […ôz ΥàΈ≤a] βÄî but un glop [u≈΄ Υà…Γl…îp] keeps the stop after the nasal. /d/ additionally resists lenition after a lateral, so its left context excludes βü®lβü©.

sandhi_rules.name: external-spirantization-g
sandhi_rules.notes: PHRASE-LEVEL (cross-word) SPIRANTIZATION: Catalan lenition of /b d …Γ/ is not word-bounded βÄî inside a phonological phrase an initial /b d …Γ/ surfaces as the approximant [Έ≤ ΟΑ …Θ] whenever the preceding word ends in a continuant (a vowel, glide, liquid or fricative), and stays a stop after a pause, a nasal or another stop (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß5.2, ¬ß10.5; Recasens 1993): la seva germana [l…ô ΥàseΈ≤…ô  £…ôrΥàman…ô], de decidir [ΟΑ…ô ΟΑ…ôsiΥàΟΑi], es va […ôz ΥàΈ≤a] βÄî but un glop [u≈΄ Υà…Γl…îp] keeps the stop after the nasal. /d/ additionally resists lenition after a lateral, so its left context excludes βü®lβü©.

stress.notes: Catalan accentuation per the Institut d'Estudis Catalans (IEC) norm (GramΟ†tica de la llengua catalana, ch. 3; Wheeler 2005 ¬ß3.1). A written accent (Ο† Ο® Ο© Ο≠ Ο≤ Ο≥ ΟΚ) always wins and marks the stressed vowel. Unmarked words are OXYTONE when they end in a consonant other than -s-preceded-by-a-vowel and other than -en/-in βÄî including the consonant+s plurals (-ns, -rs, -ts, -nts, -ps βÄΠ), which are oxytone because the plural preserves the stress of the singular (important βÜ£ importants). A word ending in a falling DIPHTHONG (-ai -au -ei -eu -iu -oi -ou) is oxytone (remei [r…ôΥàm…¦j], correu [kuΥàr…¦w], avui […ôΥàΈ≤uj]) βÄî the diphthong is one syllable, so the word does not end in a bare vowel. Unmarked words are PAROXYTONE when they end in a (single) vowel, in a vowel + -s (-as, -es, -is, -os, -us) or in -en/-in (semblen, parlin). The diaeresis βü®Ο· ΟΦβü© marks hiatus/pronounced βü®uβü©, NOT stress, so it is deliberately absent from marked_vowels (segΟΦent is [s…ôΥà…Θwen], not [s…ôΥà…Θwent] with stress on βü®ΟΦβü©). Proparoxytones always carry a written accent, so the unmarked default is paroxytone. The diphthongs list is the Catalan inventory of tautosyllabic vowel sequences (Wheeler 2005 ¬ß2.5; IEC grammar ch. 4): the falling diphthongs βü®ai au ei eu iu oi ou uiβü© and the rising βü®ua ue uo uiβü© after βü®g/qβü©. Every other vowel sequence is HIATUS and counts as two syllables βÄî te-ni-a, ve-Ο·-na, di-a βÄî which is what puts the stress, and therefore the unstressed-vowel reduction, on the right vowel.

notes: OLD CATALAN (catalΟ† antic, roughly the 11thβÄ™15th c.) βÄî the data-bearing COMMON CORE of the Catalan diasystem and the parent every modern variety inherits from. It exists because the modern varieties did NOT descend from modern Central Catalan: the Eastern/Western split predates the modern standard, and Balearic descends from the Eastern (EmpordΟ†) settlers of the 13th-century conquest, not from Barcelona. Making a dialect a child of Central Catalan asserts a false history, and the false history then generates false phonology βÄî an inherited rule can be overridden by id but not switched off, so Balearic and Valencian were forced to simplify final clusters exactly as Central does. Here the Central innovations live in ca and nowhere else, and each variety declares its own. What this node carries is (a) the pan-Catalan grapheme table, (b) the conservative Old-Catalan phonology on every point where the modern varieties diverge βÄî the seven-vowel system with NO unstressed reduction, /v/ distinct from /b/, the affricate /d £/ for βü®jβü© and βü®gβü© before a front vowel, the glide of βü®ixβü© = [j É], and word-final βü®-rβü© still pronounced (Moll 2006; Rasico 1982; Veny 1982 ch. 1) βÄî and (c) the allophony and sandhi genuinely common to ALL varieties: post-continuant spirantization of /b d …Γ/ with pre-lateral gemination blocking it, final-obstruent devoicing, nasal place assimilation, word-final βü®-igβü© = [t É], βü®xβü©/βü®ex-βü©, cross-word voicing assimilation and phrase-level lenition, and PRE-CONSONANTAL cluster simplification. It is authored against the MODERN orthography (which is deliberately etymological and largely continuous with the medieval one), so its stress block is the modern IEC orthographic accentuation; medieval manuscripts are unaccented and this node is not a spec for reading them. Not modelled, deliberately: the medieval affricates /ts dz/ for βü®c/Οßβü© and βü®tzβü©, which are lost in every modern variety, and the apico-alveolar sibilants, which only Valencian still keeps distinct.

sources.author: Moll, F. de B.
sources.title: GramΟ†tica histΟ≤rica catalana
sources.notes: The historical grammar of Catalan (Catalan edition of the 1952 GramΟΓtica histΟ≥rica catalana). The source for the Old-Catalan stage encoded in ca-x-medieval: the seven-vowel system with NO unstressed reduction, /v/ still distinct from /b/, the affricate /d £/ for βü®jβü© and βü®gβü© before a front vowel, word-final βü®-rβü© still pronounced, and the final clusters βü®-nt -lt -mp -ncβü© still intact βÄî i.e. every point on which the modern varieties diverge. Pages not verified.

sources.author: Rasico, P. D.
sources.title: Estudis sobre la fonologia del catalΟ† preliterari
sources.publisher: Curial / Publicacions de l'Abadia de Montserrat
sources.notes: Phonology of pre-literary and early Old Catalan; used for the dating of the Old-Catalan stage (the language is already differentiated from Occitan and Latin in the 11thβÄ™12th-century documents) and for the consonant system it inherits. Pages not verified.

sources.author: Badia i Margarit, A. M.
sources.title: GramΟ†tica de la llengua catalana: descriptiva, normativa, diatΟ≤pica, diacrΟ≤nica
sources.notes: Descriptive, normative, diatopic and diachronic grammar of Catalan; the cross-check used here for the consonant and vowel inventories, the stress rules and the dialect isoglosses. Pages not verified.

sources.author: Wheeler, M. W.
sources.title: The Phonology of Catalan
sources.notes: The reference phonology of Catalan and the primary authority for this spec. Cited by SECTION, not page: ¬ß2.3 vowel systems and the unstressed reduction of the Eastern block (reduction is stress-conditioned; the Western block does not reduce); ¬ß2.5 the diphthong inventory; ¬ß3.1 stress and its orthographic marking; ¬ß5.1 the consonant inventory, βü®c/gβü© before a front vowel, βü®xβü© and word-final βü®-igβü©; ¬ß5.2 spirantization of /b d …Γ/ after a continuant and the pre-lateral gemination that blocks it; ¬ß5.3 final-obstruent devoicing and cross-word voicing assimilation; ¬ß10.2βÄ™¬ß10.5 the post-lexical/phrase-level processes βÄî final-rhotic loss, final-cluster simplification, nasal place assimilation and phrase-level lenition. Section numbers are given because the printed page numbers were not verified.

sources.author: Veny, J.
sources.title: Els parlars catalans: sΟ≠ntesi de dialectologia
sources.notes: The standard dialectology of Catalan and the source for the isoglosses this spec encodes per dialect: final βü®-rβü© retained in Valencian and deleted in the rest; final-cluster simplification after a nasal everywhere but after a lateral only in the Eastern block; Western [t É] for initial βü®xβü© and [j É] for βü®ixβü©; the lleidatΟ† final βü®-aβü© = […¦]. Pages not verified.

sources.author: Recasens, D.
sources.title: FonΟ®tica i fonologia
sources.notes: Descriptive phonetics of Central Catalan: nasal place assimilation (labial before /p b m f/, velar before /k …Γ/, palato-alveolar before / É  £ t É d £  é/), spirantization and final-obstruent devoicing. Pages not verified.

sources.author: Recasens, D.
sources.title: FonΟ®tica descriptiva del catalΟ†: assaig de caracteritzaciΟ≥ de la pronΟΚncia del vocalisme i consonantisme del catalΟ† al segle XX
sources.notes: The descriptive survey of Catalan vowels and consonants across the dialects: the Western block (Valencian, North-Western) does not reduce unstressed vowels; Balearic reduces βü®aβü©/βü®eβü© to […ô] but does NOT raise unstressed βü®oβü© to [u]; the North-Western word-final βü®-aβü© = […¦]. Pages not verified.

sources.author: Institut d'Estudis Catalans
sources.title: GramΟ†tica de la llengua catalana
sources.notes: The normative grammar. Ch. 3 (l'accent) is the source for the stress block: the written accent marks the stressed vowel and always wins; unmarked words ending in a vowel, a vowel+βü®-sβü© or βü®-en/-inβü© are paroxytone and all other endings βÄî including the consonant+βü®sβü© plurals βÄî are oxytone; the diaeresis βü®Ο· ΟΦβü© marks hiatus, not stress. ¬ß6.2 and the 'mots Ο†tons' list are the source for the clitic function words (articles, contractions, atonic prepositions/conjunctions and weak pronouns) that carry no stress and therefore reduce. Cited by chapter; pages not verified.

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages
sources.notes: Cross-linguistic phonetic reference for the segment inventory symbols used here.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors

sources.notes: Accessible overview of the Old-Catalan (11thβÄ™15th c.) stage: its period, its corpus (Homilies d'OrganyΟ†, Llull, the CrΟ≤niques) and the conservative phonology βÄî no vowel reduction, /v/ distinct, final βü®-rβü© pronounced βÄî that the modern varieties diverge from.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Catalan phonology (Wikipedia)
sources.notes: Accessible overview of the vowel and consonant inventories, the Eastern/Western reduction split, spirantization, final devoicing and final-cluster simplification; useful as a cross-check on the primary sources above.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors

sources.notes: Accessible overview of the graphemeβÜ£sound correspondences encoded in the graphemes table: βü®llβü©, βü®nyβü©, βü®l¬Ζlβü©, βü®txβü©, βü®tg/tjβü©, βü®ixβü©, word-final βü®-igβü©, βü®Οßβü©, βü®qu/guβü© and the diaeresis.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Catalan, reused: Old Catalan has no Glottolog languoid of its own (glottolog_code is therefore null rather than a guessed code), and the medieval language was spoken over the same area.
